title: [BUG] "1/1/1900" produces incorrect Date value
author: nxn
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

Excel dates set to "1/1/1900" are incorrectly producing Date objects with a value of 1899-12-31T00:00:00.000Z.

Lib version: 4.3.0

## Steps To Reproduce

I have created a demo of this bug available here: https://codesandbox.io/s/naughty-cohen-02j2g?file=/src/index.js

The following excel file can be used to illustrate this issue when the value of cell A2 is read:
[1900.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/7705620/1900.xlsx)

Alternatively, create an excel file and enter "1/1/1900" into any cell (excel will format the cell as a date), then use Exceljs to read that cell value.


## The expected behaviour:

"1/1/1900" should result in a Date object with a value of 1900-01-01T00:00:00.000Z.

comments.author: nxn
comments.body:
Did a bit of research: It seems that Excel intentionally included a leap year bug from Lotus 1-2-3 for compatibility reasons. Any dates prior to March 1st 1900 are off by one because Excel behaves as if February 29th 1900 existed.

https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/office/troubleshoot/excel/wrongly-assumes-1900-is-leap-year

[1900_LeapDay.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/7706549/1900_LeapDay.xlsx)
